title: Trump’s Iran Approach Sows Confusion
summary: President Trump’s pendulum swings on Iran have often seemed driven by mood and moment rather than any discernible strategy.

title: In Strait of Hormuz Standoff, U.S. and Iran Deploy Economic Warfare
summary: The American blockade and Iran’s control of the Strait of Hormuz have created a stalemate that is neither peace nor raging conflict, with both sides attempting economic strangulation on the water.

title: U.S. and Iran Draft Cease-Fire Extension
summary: Plus, the Scripps National Spelling Bee. Here’s the latest at the end of Thursday.

title: ‘Both Parties Kind of Get It Wrong’: The Young Men Who May Swing the Midterms
summary: Many Gen Z men who voted for Donald Trump are dismayed by his time in office. But they say they are not hearing an appealing pitch from Democrats, either.

title: Swallowing Concerns, G.O.P. Senators Rally Behind Paxton
summary: After attacking the Texas attorney general, who won the G.O.P. nomination, Senate Republicans are pivoting sharply to support him in what is now a competitive race.

title: A Democrat’s Dodge on AIPAC Points to the Party’s Tensions Over Israel
summary: A key moment at a Senate primary debate in Michigan showed how pro-Israel Democrats are approaching the issue with caution.

title: Michigan Governor Gretchen Whitmer Will Not Run for President in 2028
summary: The Michigan governor made the remarks to a reporter on Thursday morning. Later, she sounded less definitive. Ms. Whitmer has been viewed as part of a field of possible Democratic candidates.

title: In Argentina, U.S. Tech Billionaire Peter Thiel Finds An Escape
summary: The billionaire’s new roots in Argentina are said to be partly motivated by concerns about the future of the United States and shared beliefs with Argentina’s right-wing leader.

title: I Tried to Sell My House With A.I.
summary: Over five frantic days, I gambled my family’s life savings on a hunch that A.I. could outperform a real estate agent.

title: Treasury Prepares to Make Trump the Face of a New $250 Bill
summary: Treasury Secretary Bessent backed the idea, which would require legislation allowing a living person to appear on currency.

title: Dell Gets a $9.7 Billion Defense Contract. Trump’s Portfolio Stands to Benefit.
summary: The president’s family says his stock purchases are automatically timed, but recent acquisitions of the computer company’s shares raise questions.

title: Iran, Gas Prices, the Ballroom: Trump Professes Indifference Over Fallout
summary: President Trump has increasingly adopted a posture of nonchalance when pushed on the political consequences of his actions.

title: ABC Accuses Trump Administration of Threatening Its Broadcast Licenses
summary: The TV network slammed the Federal Communications Commission, saying the regulator was targeting its station licenses in a campaign of “unconstitutional retaliation.”

title: Bari Weiss Names Nick Bilton to Lead CBS’s ‘60 Minutes’ in Major Shake-Up
summary: Bari Weiss, CBS’s editor in chief, named Nick Bilton, a tech journalist and filmmaker, as the show’s executive producer. The network also fired two on-air correspondents.

title: Two Papers Named ‘Star’ Will Duel in D.C. to Win Readers From The Washington Post
summary: First, The Star was born. Now, The Washington Star is reborn. Both are hoping to win readers ceded by recent layoffs at The Washington Post.

title: Curating in the Cross Hairs: Is This the Smithsonian Chief’s Last Show?
summary: Lonnie G. Bunch III, the Smithsonian secretary under pressure from the White House, organized an exhibit exploring America’s founding ideals.

title: Suit Says Black Infants Were Subjected to Experimental Vaccine Without Consent
summary: The babies were part of a vaccine trial for a respiratory virus in the 1960s and died shortly after. Their families said they had been unaware of the trial until recently.

title: After an Explosion at a Washington Paper Mill, Mourning Mixes With Dread
summary: The disaster at the mill in Longview, Wash., which took at least eight lives, was greeted with sadness but not surprise by a community that knew cutbacks and other pressures had raised risks.

title: Paul McCartney on His New Album and the Beatles’ Earliest Days
summary: On his new album, the ex-Beatle revisits his past and revels in simply continuing to create.

title: With Pitchford v. Cain, The Supreme Court Protected Jury Rights. Now It Must Go Further.
summary: All Americans benefit from the outcome of this case. And yet the decision highlights just how much work still needs to be done.

title: Our Military Is Built for the Wrong Century
summary: Ukraine and Iran have shown us that war as we’ve known it is over.

title: Scooter Rider and Cyclist Are Killed in Head-On Crash on N.Y.C. Bridge
summary: The collision on the Queensboro Bridge involved an e-scooter that is not legally allowed to be used on the streets of New York.

title: Terry Rozier Took $70,000 Bribe to Leave NBA Game Early, U.S. Says
summary: Terry Rozier faces new charges for his role in a betting scheme that prosecutors say involves former and current professional basketball players.

title: Alaska Authorities Race to Rescue 4 Climbers Who Fell on Mount McKinley
summary: The condition of the climbers was not immediately known.

title: Italian Police Uncover Dead Mob Boss’s $230 Million Business Empire
summary: When Matteo Messina Denaro died in 2023, many of his secrets died with him. That began to change with a tip about a wealthy Sicilian woman’s significant assets in a tiny European principality.
